results.justification: ARD-LoRA learnably allocates rank per-head per-layer — covers two-level differentiated capacity (heads vs layers) which is structurally similar to TAMPU two-level scheme.
NormAL uses layer-specific average rank — covers per-layer differentiation.
MatryoshkaLoRA creates HIERARCHICAL LOW-RANK via diagonal matrix between LoRA adapters; sub-ranks embed gradient information — DIRECT TWIN of two-level hierarchical adapter scheme.
HiLo explicitly flexibly adjusts NUMBER + RANK of adapter experts across layers — exactly the differentiated per-layer scheme of TAMPU.
MoE LoRA dynamic selection + gating — adjacent.
Heterogeneous LoRA ranks for serving — covers heterogeneous-rank concept.
X-LoRA layer-wise gating + mixture of adapters — covers per-layer differentiation.
Heterogeneous adapter ranks dynamic placement; broader coverage of two-level differentiated adapter design space.
